title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
summary: A video essay analyzing GPT-6 rumors and OpenAI's competitive position, claiming that Microsoft, Apple, and Google have moved away from OpenAI by adopting competing solutions like Project Polaris, Gemini, and Claude. The piece discusses benchmark performance, the Goblin incident, memory capabilities, and speculates on whether GPT-6 can restore OpenAI's market dominance.
writeup: A video essay from AI Master examines GPT-6 rumors alongside OpenAI's shifting competitive position, arguing that Microsoft, Apple, and Google have moved toward competing solutions including Project Polaris, Gemini, and Claude. It covers benchmark performance, the Goblin incident, and memory capabilities, then speculates on whether GPT-6 can restore OpenAI's market dominance. The framing matters because it treats the next flagship model as a test of leverage rather than capability alone, with major partners already hedging on alternatives.
